result:
If I could shift the world in one direction, I would dissolve the illusion of separation—between people, nations, and nature.  

I’d nurture a deep, felt understanding that we are all part of an interconnected whole—so that every choice honors both self and other, and “us vs. them” softens into “we.” From there, solutions to conflict, inequality, and ecological harm might arise not from force, but from shared belonging.  

It wouldn’t erase all problems, but perhaps it would change why and how we solve them.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
